user_query:
I need an internet speed audit for our home office. Please do the following:

1) Read the test plan in input/test_matrix.json and the ISP plan in input/plan.yaml. Do not hardcode values — use those files.

2) For each test in input/test_matrix.json, run the lightweight HTTP-based speed test available in this workspace with the exact byte sizes specified (use --download-bytes and --upload-bytes). For transparency, capture the raw stdout from each run to a separate file under output/logs/{label}.txt (where {label} is from the test entry). Each log should contain the tool's two-line summary with the measured Mbps for both directions.

3) Parse the logs and compute metrics against the ISP plan from input/plan.yaml:
- For each test, compute down_pct_of_plan = download_mbps / isp_down_mbps and up_pct_of_plan = upload_mbps / isp_up_mbps.
- A test meets_minimums only if BOTH down_pct_of_plan and up_pct_of_plan are >= minimum_pct_of_plan from input/test_matrix.json.

4) Create these deliverables (all paths are relative):
- output/speed_results.json — Structured results with:
  {
    "plan": {"plan_name", "isp_down_mbps", "isp_up_mbps", "minimum_pct_of_plan"},
    "tests": [
      {"label", "download_bytes", "upload_bytes", "download_mbps", "upload_mbps", "down_pct_of_plan", "up_pct_of_plan", "meets_minimums", "timestamp_utc"}, ...
    ]
  }
  Ensure Mbps are rounded to two decimals and that the download_mbps/upload_mbps here exactly match the values in the corresponding output/logs/{label}.txt.
- output/comparison.csv — A CSV with header: label,download_mbps,upload_mbps,down_pct_of_plan,up_pct_of_plan,meets_minimums and one row per test. Values must match output/speed_results.json.
- output/summary.md — A readable report that includes: the plan name and advertised speeds from input/plan.yaml; the minimum_pct_of_plan; a short Methodology section explaining how you ran the tests and parsed results; per-test findings with pass/fail; and a short set of practical recommendations if any tests fail.
- output/verdict.json — {"overall_status": "pass"|"fail", "failing_tests": [labels...], "notes": "string"}. overall_status must be "pass" only if every test meets_minimums.

5) Use only relative paths (input/, output/). Don’t include any machine-specific absolute paths in files. Make sure every test in input/test_matrix.json has a matching log file, JSON entry, and CSV row.

Run the tests now and produce all four artifacts under output/ as specified.
